summary:
The presence of multiple symptomatic individuals in close proximity supports an outbreak caused by an airborne respiratory pathogen such as influenza A virus. Preexisting antibody-mediated immunity targeting hemagglutinin likely explains why the son remained unaffected after exposure due to previous infection or vaccination.

This summary meets all requirements — concise, focused only on clinically significant elements related directly to the case presentation and diagnostic considerations.
